The paper's printed tables include the 20 fastest stars (Hivel1–Hivel20) with full unbound probabilities and other parameters, but the remaining 284 possibly unbound candidates (out of the 304 with P_ub(X)>50%) cannot be individually identified from the text and provided assets alone. Therefore, no individual candidate records are extracted.",

    {
      "id": "step-09",
      "depends_on": ["step-08"],
      "step_type": "radial_velocity_measurement",
      "summary": "Adopt LASP radial velocities for the 591 HiVelSCs and apply a zero-point correction of -5.4 km/s, derived from cross-match with a RV standard star catalog.",

      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex",
          "start_line": 280,
          "end_line": 288,
          "context": "LASP RV adoption and zero-point correction"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-10",
      "depends_on": ["step-04", "step-05", "step-09"],
      "step_type": "velocity_calculation",
      "summary": "Final velocity calculation: recompute Galactocentric positions and velocities for the 591 HiVelSCs using the corrected LASP RVs, Gaia astrometry and solar parameters. The recalculated V_GC values are all > 445 km/s.",
      "inputs": ["591 HiVelSCs", "corrected RVs", "heliocentric distances", "solar parameters"],
      "outputs": ["final Galactocentric positions and velocities"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex",
          "start_line": 288,
          "end_line": 289,
          "context": "recalculate V_GC after zero-point correction"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-11",
      "depends_on": [],
      "step_type": "galactic_potential_model",
      "summary": "Adopt seven Galactic potential models for escape velocity estimation: Paczynski+1990, Gnedin+2005, Xue+2008, Koposov+2010, Kenyon+2014, MWPotential2014, Watkins+2019.",
      "inputs": [],
      "outputs": ["set of potential models"],
      "parameters": [
        {
          "name": "potential_name",
          "raw_value": "Seven models (Paczynski+1990, Gnedin+2005, Xue+2008, Koposov+2010, Kenyon+2014, MWPotential2014, Watkins+2019)",
          "value": "Seven models (Paczynski+1990, Gnedin+2005, Xue+2008, Koposov+2010, Kenyon+2014, MWPotential2014, Watkins+2019)",
          "description": "list of Galactic potential models used for escape velocities",
          "source_refs": [
            {
              "kind": "text",
              "path": "literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex",
              "start_line": 275,
              "end_line": 276,
              "context": "seven potential models"
            }
          ]
        }
      ],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex",
          "start_line": 275,
          "end_line": 276,
          "context": "models listed"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-12",
      "depends_on": ["step-10", "step-11"],
      "step_type": "escape_or_bound_assessment",
      "summary": "For each of the 591 HiVelSCs, compute the escape velocity at its position under each potential model and the unbound probability P_ub (fraction of 1000 MC realizations with V_GC > v_esc).",
      "inputs": ["final Galactocentric positions/velocities", "potential models"],
      "outputs": ["escape velocities", "unbound probabilities P_ub"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex",
          "start_line": 478,
          "end_line": 485,
          "context": "escape velocities and P_ub definition"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-13",
      "depends_on": ["step-10", "step-11"],
      "step_type": "orbit_integration",
      "summary": "Perform orbit integrations backwards in time for 10 Gyr using Galpy with the MWPotential2014 model, via 1000 MC realizations per star. Compute orbital eccentricity e, maximum height Zmax, minimum crossing radius r_min, and energy E-Phi(∞).",
      "inputs": ["final Galactocentric positions/velocities", "MWPotential2014"],
      "outputs": ["e, Zmax, r_min, E-Phi(∞)"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex",
          "start_line": 653,
          "end_line": 659,
          "context": "orbit integration with Galpy"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-14",
      "depends_on": ["step-12", "step-13"],
      "step_type": "origin_assessment",
      "summary": "Compute Pgc (fraction of orbits with r_min < 1 kpc) and PMW (r_min < 25 kpc). Classify each star into HVS, HRS, RS or OUT using thresholds on Pgc, PMW and P_ub (see Table 4).",
      "inputs": ["r_min", "P_ub"],
      "outputs": ["Pgc", "PMW", "Origin_Class"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex",
          "start_line": 703,
          "end_line": 706,
          "context": "origin probability definitions and classification"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-15",
      "depends_on": ["step-08"],
      "step_type": "stellar_parameter_inference",
      "summary": "Derive atmospheric parameters: LASP (Teff, log g, [Fe/H]) and DD-Payne (Teff, log g, [Fe/H], [α/Fe]) for the 591 HiVelSCs.",
      "inputs": ["591 HiVelSCs", "LAMOST spectra"],
      "outputs": ["stellar parameters"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex",
          "start_line": 596,
          "end_line": 601,
          "context": "LASP and DD-Payne atmospheric parameters"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "step-16",
      "depends_on": ["step-14"],
      "step_type": "candidate_classification",
      "summary": "Record the final origin classification (HVS, HRS, RS, OUT) in the catalog.",
      "inputs": ["Origin classification results"],
      "outputs": ["Origin_Class column in catalog"],
      "parameters": [],
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex",
          "start_line": 706,
          "end_line": 707,
          "context": "counts of 91 HVS, 107 HRS, 221 RS, 172 OUT"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "candidates": [],
  "candidate_groups_considered": [
    {
      "group_id": "sample-591",
      "description": "The full set of 591 HiVelSCs selected from LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2, as described in the paper.",
      "decision": "source_missing",
      "reason": "The individual star data, including the unbound probabilities (P_ub) under seven potential models needed to determine which are possibly unbound from the Galaxy, are only available in an external CSV file not present in the local assets. The paper's printed tables provide information for only the 20 fastest candidates; the remaining 284 possibly unbound candidates (out of the 304 with P_ub(X)>50%) cannot be identified without the external CSV.",
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex",
          "start_line": 106,
          "end_line": 106,
          "context": "abstract stating 591 HiVelSCs"
        },
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex",
          "start_line": 278,
          "end_line": 279,
          "context": "final selection of 591 HiVelSCs after manual RV check"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "group_id": "top-20-fastest",
      "description": "The 20 fastest HiVelSCs (Hivel1 through Hivel20), which are a subset of the possibly unbound candidates, with full printed tables of basic parameters, kinematic positions/velocities, escape velocities, unbound probabilities, and atmospheric parameters.",
      "decision": "incomplete",
      "reason": "These 20 stars are confirmed to have P_ub > 0.5 in all seven potential models, making them unbound in the most conservative sense, but they represent only a small fraction of the total 304 candidates that are possibly unbound (P_ub(X) > 0.5). The full set of 304 cannot be identified without the external CSV.",
      "source_refs": [
        {
          "kind": "text",
          "path": "literature/2011.10206/arxiv_source/LAMOST_II_HVS.tex",

        },

message.reasoning_content:
We need to produce the JSON with the skeleton plus method_chain, candidate_groups_considered, and roster of candidates with identifiers only. The paper is about 591 high velocity star candidates (HiVelSCs). The paper defines a sample of 591 HiVelSCs, which are high velocity stars selected from LAMOST DR7 and Gaia DR2, with total velocities in the Galactic rest frame larger than 445 km/s. It discusses unbound probabilities. Among them, 43 have Pub > 50% in Watkins+2019, 304 in Xue+2008, etc. The paper's classification divides them into HVS, HRS, RS, OUT candidates based on Pgc, PMW, Pub thresholds. The paper explicitly treats many as possibly unbound. Indeed, the abstract says "at least 43 HiVelSCs are unbound to the Galaxy with escape probabilities larger than 50%". So these are HVS/unbound candidates. The paper defines all 591 as "high velocity star candidates" and investigates which are bound/unbound. Many are "possibly unbound". According to the extraction skill, we should include objects that the paper treats as possibly unbound from the Milky Way / Galactic potential. The paper considers all 591 as high velocity star candidates, but not all are necessarily treated as "possibly unbound". The paragraph in Section 2.4 (lines 482-485) says: "at least 43 HiVelSCs have Pub > 50% of being unbound in Watkins+2019 potential model, and at most 304 HiVelSCs have Pub > 50% of being unbound in Xue+2008 potential model, and the amount of unbound HiVelSCs with Pub > 50% in other potential models is given by a number that is within the range (43, 304)". It also later says "there are at least 43 stars ... that can escape ... most conservative Watkins+2019 ... with Pub > 50%, and 287 stars are bound in the least conservative potential model of Xue+2008 with Pub ≤ 50%. Whether the remaining 261 stars are marginally unbound with Pub > 50% or not depends on the potential model used." So the paper treats 287 as bound even in Xue+2008, i.e., definitely not unbound; 304 as unbound in Xue+2008, and 43 as unbound in the most conservative model. The remaining 261 are ambiguous. The paper itself says that at most 304 are unbound, and 287 are bound (in Xue+2008). It's clear that the paper considers all 591 as "high velocity star candidates", but not all as possibly unbound? Actually, the selection criteria (Section 2.3) used to select the 591: "31,440 samples are picked out with the condition of either escaping from our Galaxy under at least one potential model or bound but at least with total velocities of V_GC ≥ 450 km/s as used in Marchetti+2019 to select HiVelSCs." So the sample includes stars that could be unbound in some model or have high V_GC. So the paper's sample includes some objects that were originally flagged as possibly escaping under some potential model, but after further analysis, many are bound even in the optimistic model? Wait, the selection used "either escaping from our Galaxy under at least one potential model or bound but at least with total velocities of V_GC ≥ 450 km s^{-1}". The subsequent analysis then computed unbound probabilities under seven models. The results show that under the most conservative model, only 43 have Pub > 50% unbound; under the least conservative, 304; and the remaining 287 are bound even in that optimistic model. So the paper has already determined that a subset is bound. But does the paper treat all 591 as possibly unbound from the Milky Way? The paper's title: "591 high velocity stars ..." and it refers to them as "high velocity star candidates (HiVelSCs)". The inclusion basis for our extraction: we need to include objects that the paper treats as "possibly unbound from the Milky Way / Galactic potential". Using the paper's own classification: the 304 with Pub > 50% in Xue+2008 are considered unbound in that model, but the paper might treat them as "possibly unbound" even though they are bound in other models; the 43 are unbound in all models. The 287 with Pub ≤ 50% in Xue+2008 are considered bound even in the most optimistic model, so they are not possibly unbound. However, the paper mentions that some of those 287 could still be marginally unbound in other models? Actually, it says "the remaining 261 stars are marginally unbound with Pub > 50% or not depends on the potential model used." 261 = 304 - 43? Wait, 304 unbound in Xue+2008, 43 of which are also unbound in Watkins+2019. So 304-43=261 are unbound in Xue+2008 but bound in Watkins+2019. So 261 are "possibly unbound" depending on model. The 287 are bound in Xue+2008, i.e., even the most optimistic model says they are bound, so they are not treated as possibly unbound. So according to the paper's own analysis, the set of stars that are possibly unbound (i.e., have Pub > 50% in at least one model) is the union of those with Pub > 50% in any model, which is at most 304 (Xue+2008), but includes also those with Pub > 50% in other models that might be bound in Xue+2008? Actually, if a star has Pub > 50% in Xue+2008, it's unbound in that model, but if it's bound in Xue+2008 (Pub ≤ 50%), it could still have Pub > 50% in other models? The escape velocity is model-dependent; if it's bound in the least conservative model (Xue+2008 has smallest escape velocities), then it would be more bound in other models with larger escape velocities (since escape velocity is higher, making it harder to be unbound). So if a star is bound in Xue+2008 (Pub ≤ 50%), it cannot have Pub > 50% in any other model because those models have larger escape velocities, making unbound probability lower. So the set of possibly unbound stars is exactly those with Pub(X) > 50%? But wait, the paper says "at most 304 HiVelSCs have Pub > 50% of being unbound in Xue+2008". That's the highest number. If a star has Pub ≤ 50% in Xue+2008, it is bound in all models because Xue+2008 is the model with the smallest escape velocities, so if it's bound there, it's bound everywhere. So the 287 are bound in all potential models, i.e., they are definitively Galaxy-bound. So the paper does not treat those as possibly unbound. The remaining 304 have Pub > 50% in Xue+2008 (the most optimistic), and some are also unbound in other models, but they are at least unbound in that model.
